req.title: Consent management (negative)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers consent grant/revoke lifecycle on the /Consent FHIR endpoint with strict SLA enforcement (15 min cache invalidation). Tests are derived from: (1) EP – valid grant payload succeeds; (2) NEG – missing mandatory 'patient' element returns 422; (3) STC – active→revoked state transition blocks subsequent reads within SLA window; (4) DT – conflicting state transition (revoke already-inactive consent) is rejected; (5) BVA – access attempt at exactly the 15-minute SLA boundary is denied. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and teardown consent resources.

tests.name: test_consent_grant_valid_patient
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
FHIR-Lite service is running and reachable
No pre-existing consent record for the test patient
actions: POST /Consent with a valid FHIR Consent resource body including status=active, patient reference, and scope
Assert HTTP 201 Created
Assert response body contains id and status=active
expected: Location header references the created Consent resource
tests.executable:
def test_consent_grant_valid_patient(api_client, patient_auth_token, consent_cleanup):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Consent",
        "status": "active",
        "scope": {"coding": [{"code": "patient-privacy"}]},
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "59284-0"}]}],
        "patient": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "dateTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        "provision": {"type": "permit"}
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}"}
    resp = api_client.post("/Consent", json=payload, headers=headers)
    consent_cleanup(resp)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert body.get("id")
    assert "Location" in resp.headers

tests.name: test_consent_grant_missing_patient_reference
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
FHIR-Lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Consent with a payload that omits the mandatory 'patient' field
Assert HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Assert response body contains OperationOutcome with issue referencing 'patient'
expected: At least one issue with diagnostics mentioning 'patient'
trace: R-HLT-065: missing mandatory elements must be tested

tests.name: test_revoked_consent_enforced_at_sla_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
Authenticated third-party accessor token available via <accessor_auth_token> fixture
Active consent record exists for the test patient (created by <create_active_consent> fixture)
System clock is controllable or test uses <advance_time> helper
actions: PATCH /Consent/{id} to set status=inactive (revoke)
Assert HTTP 200 OK on revoke
Advance simulated time to exactly 15 minutes after revoke using <advance_time>
GET patient data resource as third-party accessor
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden at exactly the 15-minute boundary
expected: GET at T+15min returns HTTP 403 Forbidden
trace: R-HLT-065: revoked consent enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-065: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

tests.name: test_revoke_already_inactive_consent_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
An existing consent record with status=inactive exists via <create_inactive_consent> fixture
actions: PATCH /Consent/{id} attempting to set status=inactive on an already-inactive consent
Assert HTTP 409 Conflict or 422 Unprocessable Entity
Assert response body contains OperationOutcome describing conflicting state
expected: Issue diagnostics reference invalid state transition
trace: R-HLT-065: conflicting state transitions must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_revoke_already_inactive_consent_rejected(
    api_client, patient_auth_token, create_inactive_consent
):
    consent_id = create_inactive_consent(patient_id="test-patient-002")
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"
    }
    resp = api_client.patch(f"/Consent/{consent_id}", json=patch_body, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert issues, "Expected at least one OperationOutcome issue"

tests.name: test_consent_state_transition_active_to_revoked
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
Active consent record exists via <create_active_consent> fixture
FHIR-Lite service is running and reachable
actions: GET /Consent/{id} to confirm initial status=active
PATCH /Consent/{id} to transition status from active to inactive
GET /Consent/{id} again to confirm status=inactive
Assert the full active→inactive transition is reflected in resource history
expected: GET /Consent/{id}/_history contains both active and inactive versions
tests.executable:
def test_consent_state_transition_active_to_revoked(
    api_client, patient_auth_token, create_active_consent
):
    consent_id = create_active_consent(patient_id="test-patient-003")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_auth_token}"}
    get1 = api_client.get(f"/Consent/{consent_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get1.status_code == 200
    assert get1.json().get("status") == "active"
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "inactive"}]
    patch_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/Consent/{consent_id}", json=patch_body,
        headers={**headers, "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200
    assert patch_resp.json().get("status") == "inactive"
    get2 = api_client.get(f"/Consent/{consent_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get2.status_code == 200
    assert get2.json().get("status") == "inactive"
    hist = api_client.get(f"/Consent/{consent_id}/_history", headers=headers)
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    statuses = [e["resource"]["status"] for e in hist.json().get("entry", [])]
    assert "active" in statuses and "inactive" in statuses

tests.name: test_consent_access_denied_without_permission
preconditions: An unauthenticated (no token) or non-patient-role request context is available
FHIR-Lite service is running and reachable
actions: POST /Consent with a valid payload but no Authorization header
Assert HTTP 401 Unauthorized
POST /Consent with a valid payload but using a <non_patient_token> (e.g., practitioner role)
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden
expected: No consent resource created in either case
trace: R-HLT-065: permission denial must be tested
